summary: Moved the dolphin/os.h include in src/placeholder.h out of conditional preprocessor branches so m2ctx/decomp.me context generation can see it reliably. The PR body notes that includes inside #if blocks were not handled properly, with only the first include being expanded, and this header appeared to be the relevant case.
what_changed: Added #include <dolphin/os.h> at top-level in src/placeholder.h, immediately after #include <m2c_macros.h>.
Removed duplicate conditional #include <dolphin/os.h> from the __clang__ / __GNUC__ NOT_IMPLEMENTED branch.
Removed duplicate conditional #include <dolphin/os.h> from the final fallback NOT_IMPLEMENTED branch.
Kept the NOT_IMPLEMENTED macro behavior intact while making the OSPanic declaration available without depending on conditional include expansion.
smart_moves: Fixed the m2ctx/decomp.me context issue by hoisting an include rather than changing macro definitions or platform-specific behavior.
Eliminated duplicated conditional includes and replaced them with one unconditional include, reducing context-generation ambiguity.
Preserved the existing conditional NOT_IMPLEMENTED cases for clang/GNUC, M2CTX, MWERKS non-BUGFIX, and fallback compilation paths.
decomp_lessons: For headers consumed by m2ctx/decomp.me, avoid placing required includes only inside #if branches when the tool may not expand all conditional includes correctly.
If a macro uses a symbol such as OSPanic across multiple branches, prefer making the required declaration visible at a stable top-level include location.
Small header hygiene changes can unblock decomp.me context generation without touching matching code.
naming_conventions: The placeholder.h helper typedefs use jmp_t for a jump-table label function pointer and jtbl_t for an array of jump labels.
The NOT_IMPLEMENTED macro remains the central placeholder macro for unimplemented function stubs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was introduced.
The change supports decomp.me/m2ctx setup by ensuring OSPanic is available in generated context, which can help scratches compile consistently.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
PR body feedback from the author states that includes inside #if blocks were currently not handled properly, with only the first #include expanded, motivating the hoist.
follow_up_queries: Find other headers where required includes are placed only inside #if branches and may confuse m2ctx.
Search for NOT_IMPLEMENTED users that rely on OSPanic availability through placeholder.h.
Audit decomp.me context failures involving conditional #include expansion.
